title: Better Animal Feeding
description: Feeding mobs in confined spaces is now easier.
body:
# ![Picture](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/05dd873d5a6c53854b7da6c286e1b6e3bc11b2cc.png)🐄 Better Animal Feeding – Breeding Made Simple!
Requirements: **Fabric API**

## ⚡ About the Mod
Tired of struggling to feed animals in cramped farms? Better Animal Feeding solves one of Minecraft's most frustrating problems: feeding animals that are stacked together!

When you click on an already-fed, breeding, or baby animal with food, the mod automatically forwards it to the next unfed animal nearby. No more fighting with hitboxes, no more missed clicks – just smooth, efficient farming!

_✅ Perfect for:_

- Compact animal farms (24+ animals in a 1×1 space!)
- Mass breeding sessions
- Players who value their time
  
## 🎯 How It Works
**Vanilla Problem:**
You have 20 cows in a pen. You click the first one with wheat → it eats. You click again → still the same cow. You move your mouse slightly → nope, still blocked. You angle your view → finally, the second cow! 😤

- With Better Animal Feeding:
You click ANY cow with wheat → it eats. You click the same cow again → food automatically goes to the next unfed cow. You keep clicking → all 20 cows are fed in seconds! 🎉

## 🔧 Features
_Smart Food Forwarding:_

- Click on already-fed animals → food goes to unfed ones
- Click on breeding animals → continues feeding others
- Click on baby animals → skips to adults

## Zero Conflicts:

- Doesn't change vanilla breeding mechanics
- Compatible with other farming mods
- Wolves, cats, and other tameable animals work as normal

## ❤️ Why You'll Love It

- **Save time –** Feed 20+ animals in seconds, not minutes
  
- **Less frustration –** No more wrestling with overlapping hitboxes
  
- **Perfect for farms –** Designed for compact, efficient breeding setups
  
- **Lightweight –** Just one simple mixin, no performance impact

## 📋 Perfect For:

- **Efficient Farmers –** Maximize your breeding efficiency
  
- **Base Builders –** Compact farms that actually work
  
- **Anyone** who's ever rage-quit trying to feed animals in a crowded pen!
